_comment: Power-plan tweaks applied via powercfg /setacvalueindex + /setdcvalueindex on the ACTIVE plan (SCHEME_CURRENT). Every recipe: id, powercfgSubgroup (SUB_* GUID alias), powercfgSetting (setting GUID alias), ACValue and DCValue (raw powercfg value — seconds for timers, % for CPU throttle, 0/1/2 for enum), appliesTo (all / laptop / desktop / ryzen6plus_laptop / intel_11plus_laptop / modern_standby_laptop), rationale. Diagnose snapshots the active plan via powercfg /export before applying. Values documented for reference: PROCTHROTTLEMIN 5-100 (%), STANDBYIDLE 0=never else seconds, HIBERNATEIDLE 0=never else seconds, LIDACTION 0=nothing 1=sleep 2=hibernate 3=shutdown, ASPM 0=off 1=moderate 2=maximum.
modernStandbyNote: On Modern Standby (S0-Low-Power-Idle) laptops (Intel 11th-gen+, Ryzen 6000+, most Ultra 100+ machines) S3 sleep is not exposed — sleep = 'connected standby' that keeps some hardware ticking. Setting DC sleep = never on these machines is deliberate: it forces hibernate instead, which is the only true zero-power state available. Confirm 'powercfg /a' does not list S3 before applying.

recipes.rationale: PCIe Active State Power Management off. Fixes combo-card WLAN/BT wakes, NVMe stalls, and Realtek WLAN crashes on Ryzen 6000+. Sacrifices ~0.5W idle for reliability.

recipes.promptText: When you close the laptop lid, what should happen?
promptOptions: Do nothing — I'll press power to shut it down
recipes.rationale: Lid close: do nothing. Prevents Modern Standby wake-in-bag heat + battery drain. Combine with hibernate-on-timer for a true 'close lid = safe' UX. Requires prompt because 'close lid → sleep' is the expected default for most users; silently changing it surprises people who close the lid to walk away and come back to find nothing woke up.
Sleep timer: never (both AC + DC). On Modern Standby machines Windows will otherwise stay in S0LPI and drain the battery in a bag. Rely on hibernate timer instead.
Hibernate after 30 minutes on battery, never on AC. 1800 seconds = 0x00000708. Combined with sleep=never, this is the actual 'safe in bag' state on Modern Standby machines.
For S3-capable laptops, same 30-min hibernate as a longer fallback after sleep. AC never.
Minimum processor state 5%. Safe minimum — lets the CPU park cores for idle without the 0% edge cases some drivers hate.
Maximum processor state 100%. Turbo / boost allowed on both AC and DC — the OEM firmware limits real power under battery anyway.

recipes.promptText: Do any of your USB devices drop out or freeze randomly? (BT dongles, audio interfaces, external mice, dongles going unresponsive.)
promptOptions: Yes, they do — turn off USB power saving
recipes.rationale: USB selective suspend off. Prevents dropped audio interfaces, disconnected BT dongles, and the classic 'mouse dies until I unplug it' failure. Optional — costs ~0.2W idle. Requires prompt because most users don't have USB drop-out issues; turning this off preemptively costs battery for no gain.
Disk spin-down: never on AC, 10 min on DC. Modern NVMe ignores this but SATA HDDs / SSDs on old laptops still respect it.

postApply.note: Re-apply the active scheme so the setacvalueindex/setdcvalueindex changes take effect.
